label: noncommutation
headline_statement: Confirmed rate-corrected noncommutation result. Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:tail}, the pointwise-band comparator is conservative relative to the sharp nested MSM-CVaR endpoint: \(W_{\tau,\Gamma}^{\mathrm{band}}(\pi;P)\ge \underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\). In the proposed two-action, three-outcome, one-covariate-cell strict-overlap MSM response-type class with \(1<\Gamma<\infty\), the common monotone upper-tail tilt is simultaneously optimal for every generated loss \((Y-\eta)_+\), hence \(W_{\tau,\Gamma}^{\mathrm{band}}(\pi;P)=\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\) and \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)=0\) throughout that class.
lineage_note: The original strict-existence clause was refuted by the common upper-tail MSM vertex in the finite response-type LP; the mended statement corrects the claimed finite-class value from \(>0\) to \(0\), with no added hypothesis.
